core_behavior.note: FC: Models A and C omit the explicit no-network/no-DNS/no-deliverability-check statement present in Model B and required by critical behaviors.

inputs.note: Model A specifies no length constraint; Models B and C both specify a 254-character maximum length limit.

outputs.note: Model A returns only a boolean; Model B returns VALID/INVALID with optional reason codes; Model C returns a boolean with optional error message or code.

assumptions.note: Model A assumes case-insensitivity and trims whitespace; Model B assumes no trimming and treats whitespace as significant; Model C assumes case-sensitivity and treats whitespace as part of the address.

failure_modes.note: Model B adds a LENGTH_EXCEEDED failure mode (>254 chars) and Model C also adds it, while Model A does not include any length-based rejection.

edge_cases.note: Model A trims leading/trailing spaces before validation; Model B explicitly does not trim and treats spaces as part of the local part; Model C treats whitespace as part of the address causing potential failure.

security_constraints.note: Model B has an empty security_constraints array while Models A and C specify constraints against code execution and information exposure or mailbox harvesting.

performance_constraints.note: Model B has no performance constraints; Model A specifies O(1) time and 10,000 validations/second; Model C specifies under 10ms and thousands per second.

summary: CON and ASM each appeared twice as the most frequent taxonomy categories across the eight fields.
